proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 31 candidate predictors, feature_008 (binary, prevalence 0.40) and feature_013 (binary, prevalence 0.55) have the strongest univariate associations with objective_response: feature_008=1 raises the probability of response (positive Pearson correlation), and feature_013=1 lowers it (negative Pearson correlation).
feature_030 is constant (all zeros) and contributes no information about objective_response, so it can be excluded from subsequent modeling.

analyses.code: for c in df.columns: print(c, df[c].corr(df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Pearson correlation with objective_response: feature_008 r=+0.232 (largest positive), feature_013 r=-0.224 (largest negative); next largest absolute correlations are feature_015 r=-0.094, feature_022 r=-0.087, feature_001 r=-0.081, feature_021 r=-0.071. Most other features have |r| < 0.05.
feature_030 has a single unique value (0) across all 50,000 rows; constant column with zero variance, no association possible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_008=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_008=0 (positive main effect of feature_008 on response, on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression: feature_008 beta = +1.097, OR = 2.99 (95% CI ~2.86-3.13). Crude response rate is 36.1% with feature_008=1 vs 15.9% with feature_008=0 (absolute risk difference +20.2 percentage points). Highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013=1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with feature_013=0 (negative main effect on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression: feature_013 beta = -1.071, OR = 0.343 (~3-fold lower odds). Crude response rate is 15.3% with feature_013=1 vs 36.4% with feature_013=0. Highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (categorical 0/1/2) shows a monotone negative association with objective_response: higher levels of feature_001 correspond to lower response probability.
feature_022 (continuous, heavily right-skewed, range 0.12-3622) shows a monotone negative association with objective_response.
feature_015=1, feature_021=1, and feature_027=1 are each associated with lower objective_response (negative main effects).
feature_002 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_020 and feature_024 (continuous) are negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression: feature_001 beta = -0.285 per unit increase, OR per level = 0.752. Response rates by level: 0 -> 27.9%, 1 -> 23.0%, 2 -> 17.8%. Monotone decreasing.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_022', data=df).fit()  # also deciles cross-tab
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression: feature_022 beta = -0.0047 per unit, OR = 0.995 (effects accumulate over the wide range). Response rate by deciles falls monotonically from 33.6% (lowest decile) to 13.8% (highest decile).
feature_015 beta=-0.615 (OR 0.541, p=2.0e-96); feature_021 beta=-0.640 (OR 0.527, p=1.5e-54); feature_027 beta=-0.403 (OR 0.668, p=3.1e-09). All three show significant negative main effects.
feature_002 beta=+0.096 per unit, OR=1.10. Small but statistically significant positive association.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_020', data=df).fit(); smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_024', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_020 beta=-0.032 per unit (p=3.4e-29); feature_024 beta=-0.0069 per unit (p=5.6e-08). Both small negative associations, statistically robust at this sample size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The remaining binary features (feature_004, feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023) and the remaining continuous lab-like features (feature_003, feature_007, feature_009, feature_012, feature_014, feature_016, feature_018, feature_025, feature_026, feature_028, feature_029, feature_031, feature_032) and feature_010 (Gleason-like 6-10 integer) have no detectable univariate association with objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in remaining_features: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {c}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All p-values > 0.10 in univariate logistic regressions for these 20 features. Largest absolute Pearson r was 0.005. Consistent with these features being null with respect to response on their own, leaving feature_008, feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, feature_001, feature_022, feature_002, feature_020, feature_024 as the only marginally informative predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable logistic adjustment for all other features, feature_008 retains a large positive effect on objective_response (adjusted OR substantially > 1), and feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027 retain large negative effects.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit: feature_008 adj. beta=+1.20 (OR 3.32, p<1e-300); feature_013 adj. beta=-1.13 (p<1e-300); feature_021 adj. beta=-0.74 (p=8e-64); feature_015 adj. beta=-0.69 (p=1e-108); feature_027 adj. beta=-0.43 (p=2.5e-09); feature_001 adj. beta=-0.33 (p<1e-80); feature_022, feature_020, feature_024 retain small negative effects (p<1e-7); feature_002 retains small positive effect (p=2e-8). All other coefficients statistically null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong negative interaction between feature_008 and feature_013 on objective_response: the positive treatment-like effect of feature_008 is sharply attenuated (or abolished) in patients with feature_013=1 versus feature_013=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logit: beta(feature_008:feature_013) = -1.978 (p effectively 0). Stratified response rates: feature_013=0 -> 16.9% (feature_008=0) vs 60.96% (feature_008=1), risk difference +44.0 pp; feature_013=1 -> 15.05% (feature_008=0) vs 15.81% (feature_008=1), risk difference +0.8 pp (chi2 p=0.087). The treatment-like benefit of feature_008 is essentially confined to feature_013=0 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 also negatively modifies the effect of feature_008: among feature_021=1 patients, the positive feature_008 effect is much smaller than among feature_021=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logit: beta(feature_008:feature_021) = -1.138, p=3.3e-42. Stratified response: feature_021=0 -> 16.0% vs 38.5% (RD +22.5 pp); feature_021=1 -> 14.7% vs 15.4% (RD +0.6 pp). Strong negative modification of the feature_008 effect by feature_021.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 also negatively modifies the feature_008 effect: feature_015=1 patients get little to no benefit from feature_008.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logit: beta(feature_008:feature_015) = -1.237, p=1.4e-93. Stratified response: feature_015=0 -> 16.0% vs 41.1% (RD +25.1 pp); feature_015=1 -> 15.7% vs 16.5% (RD +0.85 pp). feature_015=1 essentially abolishes the feature_008 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 also negatively modifies the feature_008 effect: among the small minority of feature_027=1 patients (~3% of cohort), feature_008 confers no benefit (and even shows a non-significant negative point estimate).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logit: beta(feature_008:feature_027) = -1.283, p=1.8e-19. Stratified response: feature_027=0 -> 15.8% vs 36.7% (RD +20.9 pp); feature_027=1 -> 18.4% vs 16.2% (RD -2.2 pp). feature_027=1 abolishes (and slightly reverses) the feature_008 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 (the heavy-tailed continuous feature) reduces the size of the feature_008 effect on objective_response (negative interaction term in a logistic model with feature_008 x feature_022).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction logit: beta(feature_008:feature_022) = -0.0087 per unit, p=1.2e-72. The interaction is highly significant in the marginal model, indicating that on average a higher feature_022 is associated with a smaller feature_008 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_008 x feature_022 interaction is largely confounded by feature_013: feature_022 is far higher when feature_013=1, and conditional on feature_013 status (and the other binary modifiers), the feature_008 x feature_022 interaction loses statistical significance.

analyses.result_summary: feature_022 mean is 12.0 when feature_013=0 vs 67.8 when feature_013=1 (~6x). Within the all-modifier-negative subgroup (feature_013=feature_021=feature_015=feature_027=0), the feature_008 x feature_022 interaction shrinks to beta=-0.0007 with p=0.79 -- not significant. The marginal interaction is therefore explained by confounding with the binary modifiers, especially feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining a joint negative-modifier subgroup S = {feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0} captures the population in which feature_008 has a large positive effect on objective_response, while patients with any of these four modifiers active receive essentially no benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup S contains 15,681 patients (31.4% of cohort). Within S: response 17.21% (feature_008=0, n=9356) vs 79.78% (feature_008=1, n=6325); risk difference +62.6 pp; OR ~19.0; chi-square p effectively 0. Outside S (any modifier active, n=34,319): response 15.30% vs 16.06%; risk difference +0.77 pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint-negative subgroup S, the feature_008 effect is essentially uniform across all the remaining binary features (feature_004, feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023): no further binary modifier is needed.

analyses.code: for b in remaining_binaries: sub.groupby(b).apply(lambda d: d.groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean())
analyses.result_summary: Stratified within-subgroup feature_008 risk differences (feature_008=1 vs 0): feature_006 -> +0.626/+0.623 (0/1); feature_005 -> +0.648/+0.611; feature_023 -> +0.626/+0.626; feature_011 -> +0.629/+0.618; feature_017 -> +0.630/+0.585; feature_019 -> +0.622/+0.646; feature_004 -> +0.624/+0.658. All within-subgroup differences in treatment effect are <=0.05 in absolute terms; consistent with no additional modifier signal among these features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint-negative subgroup S, none of the continuous lab-like features (feature_003, feature_007, feature_009, feature_012, feature_014, feature_016, feature_018, feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_025, feature_026, feature_028, feature_029, feature_031, feature_032, feature_002) significantly modifies the feature_008 effect.

analyses.code: for c in continuous: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_008 * c', data=subgroup_S).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within-S interaction p-values (feature_008 x continuous): smallest is feature_026 p=0.058 (beta +0.044). All others p>0.17 (feature_014 p=0.18, feature_028 p=0.22, feature_018 p=0.34; many others > 0.4). With 17 simultaneous tests, no continuous feature meets a Bonferroni threshold; the within-subgroup treatment effect is essentially constant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (categorical 0/1/2) is a prognostic factor (lower response with higher levels) but is NOT a treatment-effect modifier of feature_008: the absolute risk difference associated with feature_008=1 is similar across feature_001 levels.
feature_010 (integer 6-10, Gleason-like) is neither a prognostic factor for objective_response nor a treatment-effect modifier of feature_008.

analyses.result_summary: Whole-cohort feature_008 risk difference by feature_001 level: 0 -> +20.7 pp; 1 -> +20.0 pp; 2 -> +19.8 pp. Risk differences are essentially constant; feature_001 is prognostic (response 27.9%/23.0%/17.8%) but does not modify the treatment effect.

Univariate feature_010 p=0.61. Whole-cohort feature_008 risk differences by feature_010 level (6,7,8,9,10): +0.201, +0.202, +0.200, +0.202, +0.225 -- essentially identical (slight uptick at level 10 with smallest n=2492 likely chance). No modifier signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An ANOVA-style joint likelihood ratio test of all four feature_008 x modifier interactions (feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027) versus a main-effects-only model is highly significant, confirming that the multi-modifier interaction structure is real and not explained by main effects alone.

analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test, 4 interaction terms: chi2 = 2946.0 on 4 df, p effectively 0. The interaction structure is far beyond chance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner with separate logistic regressions on feature_008=0 and feature_008=1 arms, followed by a regression-tree partition of the predicted individual treatment effects, will identify the same four binary modifiers (feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) as the dominant splits, with the leaf containing all four equal to 0 having the largest predicted treatment benefit.

analyses.code: T-learner: m0=LogReg(arm0), m1=LogReg(arm1); ITE = m1.predict_proba(X)[:,1] - m0.predict_proba(X)[:,1]; DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4, min_samples_leaf=500).fit(X, ITE)
analyses.result_summary: Tree top splits in order: feature_013 (root), then feature_015, feature_021, feature_027. Leaf with feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 has predicted ITE ~0.55 (largest); leaf with all three flipped to 1 has predicted ITE ~-0.10 to -0.14. No continuous variable enters the tree as a stronger split than these binary modifiers, even at depth 4. Independent confirmation of the same four-modifier subgroup discovered by interaction screening.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Given the strong negative effect of feature_013 on response in untreated patients (feature_008=0), feature_013 is also a prognostic factor in the absence of feature_008 (purely descriptive baseline effect, not just treatment-effect modification).

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_008=0 only (n=29,924): feature_013 beta = -0.137, OR=0.872, p=4e-7. Small but real prognostic effect (16.93% vs 15.05%). The dominant role of feature_013 is as a treatment-effect MODIFIER of feature_008, not as a strong baseline prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021, feature_015, and feature_027, like feature_013, are mostly treatment-effect modifiers of feature_008 with only modest prognostic effects in the absence of feature_008.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_008=0 only: feature_015 OR=0.978 (p~0.6), feature_021 OR~1 (p~0.5), feature_027 OR~1.06 (p~0.4) -- all weak/null prognostic effects. Compare with feature_008=1 stratum where these features dramatically reduce the response rate from ~80% to ~16% (multi-fold lower odds). These modifiers act predominantly through feature_008-modification rather than baseline prognosis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The cumulative number of active modifiers (k = feature_013 + feature_021 + feature_015 + feature_027) provides a clean dose-response summary: feature_008 risk difference is large only at k=0 and is approximately null for k >= 1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_008=1 vs 0 risk differences by k: k=0 -> +62.6 pp (n=15,681); k=1 -> +0.9 pp (n=25,372); k=2 -> +0.7 pp (n=8,186); k=3 -> -2.2 pp (n=745); k=4 -> -9.1 pp (n=16, very small). The treatment effect collapses as soon as any single modifier is active, consistent with each modifier being individually sufficient to suppress the feature_008 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004 -- the binary features that lacked a univariate main effect -- also do not modify the feature_008 effect on objective_response in the whole cohort.

analyses.code: for b in null_binaries: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ feature_008 * {b}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Whole-cohort feature_008 x b interaction p-values: feature_005 p=0.40; feature_006 p=0.86; feature_023 p=0.89; feature_011 p=0.023 (beta -0.106, very small); feature_017 p=0.12; feature_019 p>0.99; feature_004 p=0.95. Only feature_011 reaches nominal significance with a very small effect (RD +20.8 pp at 0 vs +18.9 pp at 1) -- not biologically meaningful and not a treatment-defining modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within subgroup S (k=0), feature_001 still has a graded prognostic effect on response: response increases with feature_008=1 across all feature_001 levels, but baseline response (in feature_008=0) is highest at level 0 (21.3%) and lowest at level 2 (11.9%).

analyses.result_summary: Within S, feature_008=0 vs feature_008=1 response: feature_001=0 -> 21.3% vs 85.9% (RD +64.6 pp, n=5511); feature_001=1 -> 15.9% vs 78.1% (RD +62.2 pp, n=7824); feature_001=2 -> 11.9% vs 70.9% (RD +59.0 pp, n=2346). Treatment benefit is large at every feature_001 level; differences across levels are modest and consistent with feature_001 being prognostic only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_002 (positively associated with response in the whole cohort) does not modify the feature_008 effect inside subgroup S (no interaction within the responsive subgroup); its main effect on response is therefore prognostic rather than predictive.

analyses.result_summary: Within S, beta(feature_008:feature_002) = -0.005, p=0.95. No interaction signal. feature_002 is purely prognostic in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis: 'Patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0 (subgroup S, ~31% of the cohort, n=15,681) experience a large positive effect of feature_008 on objective_response (response 17.2% with feature_008=0 vs 79.8% with feature_008=1; absolute risk difference +62.6 pp; OR ~19). Patients with any of these four modifiers active experience essentially no benefit from feature_008 (risk difference < 1 pp, on average).' This is the best-supported predictive-biomarker subgroup definition for the only outcome with usable treatment variation in this dataset.

analyses.code: Final summary: chi-square within S; logistic regression with all four interactions; LR test; T-learner+tree all converge.
analyses.result_summary: Within S: chi2 p effectively 0 for feature_008 effect; OR ~19 (95% CI roughly 17-21). Joint LR test of the 4 interactions: chi2=2946 on 4 df, p~0. Decision tree on T-learner ITE recovers feature_013/feature_015/feature_021 (and feature_027) as the dominant splits with the largest-ITE leaf at all-zero. Multiple independent analyses agree: feature_008 is the treatment-like variable, and the predictive-biomarker subgroup that defines its benefit is feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0. Effect direction: feature_008=1 INCREASES objective_response (positive sign).
